title: Change in the consumption of ozone-depleting substances
subtitle: Consumption of ozone-depleting substances, measured relative to 1986 (where consumption in 1986 is equal to 100).
note: In some years, values can be negative. This occurs when countries destroy or export gases that were produced in previous years (i.e. stockpiles).
caption:
Change in the consumption of ozone-depleting substances

Consumption of ozone-depleting substances, measured relative to 1986 (where consumption in 1986 is equal to 100).

This line chart tracks the global consumption of ozone-depleting substances from 1986 to 2021, using an index where the 1986 baseline is set to 100. The vertical axis ranges from 0 to 140, while the horizontal axis marks years in five-year increments. The data line begins at the 100 mark in 1986 and rises to a peak of approximately 130 around 1989. Following this peak, the line descends sharply, crossing the 80 mark by 1990 and dropping below 40 by 1994. A secondary, smaller rise occurs around 1998, reaching just above 20, before the downward trend resumes. From 2005 onward, the line remains in the lowest section of the chart, hovering just above the zero line through 2021. The note accompanying the chart states that in some years, values can be negative, which occurs when countries destroy or export gases produced in previous years from stockpiles. The visual data shows the line approaching the zero threshold in the final years of the timeline, consistent with the description of values potentially falling below the baseline.
